disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden eruption of intensely pruritic, firm, flat-topped violaceous polygonal papules over about 5 days, symmetrically distributed on the flexor wrists and anterior ankles, worsening over 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: What do the bumps look like — colour, shape, where are they?
When did they start and how have they changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're small firm flat-topped purple bumps, mostly on the insides of both wrists and the fronts of both ankles, matching on each side. They came up about five days ago and got much worse over the last two days.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New linear lesions appear along lines of skin trauma/scratching (Koebner phenomenon)
trigger_question_examples: Do new bumps appear where you have scratched?
Do the bumps line up in streaks anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — where I scratch, new bumps come up in a straight line a day or two later.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lacy white streaks on the buccal mucosa WITHOUT active painful ulceration or erosion, and the patient can still eat and drink normally
trigger_question_examples: Any changes inside your mouth — white patches, soreness or ulcers?
Is your mouth painful or is it stopping you eating or drinking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There are faint lacy white lines inside my cheeks, but they don't hurt, there are no raw ulcers, and I can eat and drink fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new prescription, over-the-counter, herbal or supplement medication started in the weeks before onset, and no known hepatitis C — arguing against a lichenoid drug eruption or a viral-associated trigger
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medicines, tablets, herbal remedies or supplements recently?
Any history of hepatitis or liver problems?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't started anything new at all — no tablets, herbal products or supplements — and I don't have any liver or hepatitis problems that I know of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No widespread blistering, no skin peeling or shedding, no skin pain out of proportion, no target lesions, and no fever or systemic illness — arguing against a severe cutaneous adverse reaction (SJS/TEN/DRESS/erythema multiforme)
trigger_question_examples: Any blisters, peeling skin, or skin that feels painful rather than itchy?
Any fever, feeling generally unwell, or sores in the eyes or genitals?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blisters or peeling, the skin is itchy rather than painful, and I've got no fever or feeling unwell — no sores in my eyes or anywhere else.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Deep, intractable pruritus out of proportion to the visible rash causing complete sleep deprivation for 48 hours with acute emotional exhaustion and distress
trigger_question_examples: How is the itch affecting your sleep and how you're coping?
How are you managing emotionally with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The itch is deep and maddening and I haven't slept for two days — I'm exhausted, tearful and at the end of my rope.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Nails and scalp are unaffected, and the eruption is not generalised or erosive — arguing against aggressive/scarring variants and extensive disease
trigger_question_examples: Any changes to your nails or patches of hair loss?
Is the rash spreading all over your body?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My nails look normal and my scalp is fine, and the bumps are really just on my wrists and ankles, not all over.
